topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to mock me. I stared at the 'D-'. My jaw tightened, a dull ache spreading through my temples. I crumpled the sheet slightly, the sound a small, frustrated rustle in the otherwise quiet room. "Unbelievable," I muttered, the words tasting like ash. I shoved the paper into my backpack with unnecessary force, the zipper groaning in protest. Every muscle in my neck felt coiled tight, ready to spring, though there was nowhere to actually spring toward. I just wanted the noise to stop, the expectations to dissolve into thin air.

[story 2]

I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply off the desk. My shoulders slumped, heavy and defeated. Looking at the score—a number that felt criminally low—I just let my gaze drift over the page, refusing to engage with the content. A low, almost inaudible sigh escaped my lips, a sound heavy with dissatisfaction. I picked up my pen, but instead of writing anything, I just traced the edge of the desk with my index finger, feeling the slight roughness of the wood. This whole situation was just an unnecessary imposition, a pointless drain on my limited patience.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an irritating persistence. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms so tightly my knuckles turned white. The grade stared back at me, a glaring indictment. I picked up my water bottle, took a long, deliberate sip, and set it down with a decisive clink. My eyes scanned the room, dismissing the surrounding students as irrelevant background noise. A tight knot formed deep in my stomach, a sensation of being utterly weighed down. "Fine," I said to the empty air, my voice flat and devoid of warmth. "Whatever."
